children.name: Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray

zh.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach是Gille Coemgain Macrory的女兒，於1026年之前出生在蘇格蘭。她於1050年之前與King MacBethad MacFinlay在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach是Gille Coemgain Macrory的女儿，于1026年之前出生在苏格兰。她于1050年之前与King MacBethad MacFinlay在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Mi
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 米, which means ‘rice’: (i) from one of the ‘Nine Sogdian Surnames’, also known as ‘nine surnames of Zhaowu’, because their ancestors came from Zhaowu, an ancient city in present-day Gansu province in northwestern China. During the Sui and Tang dynasties (581–987  AD ) there were nine Sogdian states in Central Asia, one of which was called Mi (米) in Chinese (located in present-day Uzbekistan). Between 649 and 655  AD , these states submitted to the Emperor Gaozong of Tang (628–683  AD ). Immigrants from the state of Mi to China acquired the surname 米. (ii) for some families, the surname is borne by descendants of Mi Fu (米芾), an artist in the Northern Song dynasty (960–1127  AD ), who claimed he was a descendant from the royal families of the state of Chu in the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) and changed his original surname 芈 to 米, a different Chinese character with the same pronunciation.

Mandarin form of the surname 宓 (also pronounced as Fu in Mandarin): (i) said to be borne by descendants of Fu Xi (伏羲), a legendary figure prior to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). (ii) said to be traced back to Mi Kang Gong (密康公, also written as 宓康公, Duke Kang of Mi, died 922  BC ), ruler of the state of Mi during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).

variants.name: Mai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Hoa, Son, Dung, Long, Binh, Minh, Thu, Hue, Huong, Thuy.  Chinese Chi, Hong, Ho, Sang, Han, Hang, Jian, Li, Man, Mei, Sen, Wei.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 麥, meaning ‘wheat’ in Chinese: (i) from Mai Qiu (麥丘), the title of a man in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), who, despite being very old, came to celebrate the birthday of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). His descendants inherited 麥, the first part of his title, as their surname. (ii) borne by descendants of Mai Tie Zhang (麥鐵杖), a famous general during the Sui dynasty (581–618  AD ). His descendants were scattered around southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 買, meaning ‘buy’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of the king Song Weizi, founder of the state of Song. (ii) borne by descendants of the Duke Dao of Xu (ruler of the state of Xu, 546–523  BC ), possibly inheriting his personal name. (iii) from the first element of the personal name Mai Zhu Chu (買朱鉏), style name of an official who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) it has also been traced back to other ethnic groups in northern China.

variants.name: Bi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Weihua, Cheng, Hui, Keping, Kun, Lei, Li Ping, Ming, Qi, Tao, Weizhen, Wenyi.  Vietnamese Chau, Hai, Hao, Hung, Mui, Nguyen, Nhung, Tran, Tuan.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 畢, originally denoting a kind of hand-net used in hunting or meaning ‘to complete’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Bi (畢), the name of a fief (located in present-day Shaanxi province), which was granted to Bi Gong Gao (畢公高, or Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chu Lian (出連) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 秘 (also pronounced as Bei or Mi in Mandarin in some local regions), meaning ‘secret’ in Chinese: surname traced back to some powerful clans in the Qiang minority ethnic group in ancient western China.

variants.name: Lua
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Jesus, Juan, Dalia, Fernando, Ignacio, Jose Luis, Josefina, Luis, Manuel, Mario.

Galician (Lúa): habitational name from a place called Lúa in Lugo province (Galicia, Spain), from the name of a river, of pre-Roman origin. This surname is apparently no longer found in Galicia (Spain).

nameMeanings.name: Lulach
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ukrainian and Rusyn: from the personal name Lukach, which is a variant of Luka (see Lucas , compare Lukac ).

Americanized form of Hungarian Lukács (see Lukacs ), Serbian, Croatian, Slovenian, Czech, Slovak, and Rusyn (from Slovakia, Serbia, and Croatia) Lukač or Lukáč (see Lukac ), and Rusyn (from Poland) Łukacz, cognates of 1 above.

variants.name: Lucas
meanings: English, German, French, Spanish, Portuguese, and Dutch: from the Latin personal name Lucas (Greek Loukas) ‘man from Lucania’. Lucania is a region of southern Italy thought to have been named in ancient times with a word meaning ‘bright’ or ‘shining’ (compare Lucio ). The Christian name owed its enormous popularity throughout Europe in the Middle Ages to Saint Luke the Evangelist, hence the development of this surname and many vernacular derivatives in most of the languages of Europe. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Loukas , Hungarian Lukács (see Lukacs ), German, Dutch, etc. Lukas , Polish Łukasz, Czech and Slovak Lukáš, Czech Lukeš, Slovenian and Croatian Lukež (see Lukes ). Compare Luke .
Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Lùcais (see McLucas ).
History: Jacques Lucas dit Lépine from Port-en-Bessin-Huppain in Calvados, France, married Françoise Capel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1653.

en.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach was born before 1026, in Scotland as the daughter of Gille Coemgain Macrory. She married King MacBethad MacFinlay before 1050, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach est née avant 1026, à Écosse, fille de Gille Coemgain Macrory. Elle a épousé King MacBethad MacFinlay avant 1050, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach wurde vor 1026 in Scotland, als Tochter von Gille Coemgain Macrory geboren. Sie hat vor 1050 in Scotland King MacBethad MacFinlay geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach è nata il prima del 1026 nel Scotland come figlia di Gille Coemgain Macrory. Lei sposato King MacBethad MacFinlay prima del 1050, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Mi Lulachは1026年以前にスコットランドでGille Coemgain Macroryの娘として生まれた。1050年以前にスコットランドでKing MacBethad MacFinlayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach(은)는 1026년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Gille Coemgain Macrory의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1050년 이전 스컷랜드에서 King MacBethad MacFinlay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach nasceu antes 1026, em Scotland filha de Gille Coemgain Macrory. Ela casou-se com King MacBethad MacFinlay antes 1050, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach родилась перед 1026 г. в местности Шотландия. Её отца звали Gille Coemgain Macrory. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени King MacBethad MacFinlay перед 1050 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mi Lulach nació antes de 1026, en Scotland como hija de Gille Coemgain Macrory. Se casó con King MacBethad MacFinlay antes de 1050, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
